blocked_by: Qdrant enabled in production (Phase 8 of az.voice migration)

context.summary: LLM conversational system with intent detection, dynamic menus, unregistered user flow. Uses OpenRouter with Grok model. All code implemented, needs production Qdrant for end-to-end testing.

subtasks.description: Audit existing handlers and identify gaps

context.summary: Map current capabilities to target requirements and identify what needs to be built vs configured

tasks: Audit qdrantHandler: per-collection support, hybrid search, LLM response generation capabilities
Audit keywordHandler: runtime registration, current commands (echo, ping, help, status)
Audit messageRouter: tag detection, dedup, routing to handlers
Audit welcomeService: per-tag welcome messages, unregistered user handling
Identify gaps: LLM menu generation, intent detection, admin-only command gating, unregistered user generic greeting
verification: Gap analysis documented with specific code locations and required changes
subtasks.output: ALREADY EXISTS: qdrantHandler has per-tag collections, hybrid search, OpenRouter LLM client, system prompts per tag. keywordHandler has echo/ping/help/status with adminOnly gating and runtime registration. messageRouter has tag/keyword detection with regex patterns. welcomeService has per-tag multi-message welcome sequences. eventRouter wires all together with auto-tag registration + welcome flow. Config has OPENROUTER_API_KEY, LLM_MODEL, OPENROUTER_BASE_URL env vars. GAPS: (1) No LLM intent detection - only regex matching in messageRouter. (2) No LLM-generated menus - qdrantHandler responses are free-form. (3) Help command is hardcoded keyword list, not dynamic tag-specific LLM guidance. (4) No unregistered user greeting - eventRouter returns no_route for unknown users. (5) No per-user stats/progress in LLM context. (6) Need LLM_MODEL set to Grok model at deploy time.

tasks: Add llmConfig to shared/config.ts with ENABLE_LLM, LLM_MODEL, API key, temperature, max tokens, brand name
Create llmService.ts with OpenAI client initialized via OpenRouter base URL
Set LLM_MODEL default to x-ai/grok-2 via env var
API key stored in WORKING-SESSION file, not in code
verification: llmConfig exported from config.ts with all required fields
llmService initializes OpenAI client with OpenRouter base URL
No API keys committed to version control
Type check passes
subtasks.output: llmConfig added to config.ts. LlmService created with OpenAI client, initialize(), isEnabled(), complete() methods. Uses same OPENROUTER_API_KEY and OPENROUTER_BASE_URL as qdrantConfig.

subtasks.description: Add LLM-based intent detection for free-text messages
tasks: Create types/llm.ts with UserIntent, IntentDetectionResult, LlmCompletionResult types
Implement detectIntent() in llmService with classification prompt returning structured JSON
Add handleUnregisteredUser() to eventRouter that calls detectIntent when user has no tags
Map tag_interest intent to auto-registration, greeting/unknown to welcome message, question to contextual response
Add parseIntentResponse() with markdown code fence stripping and graceful JSON parsing
verification: 17 unit tests pass covering all intent types, malformed JSON, API errors, code fence handling
Intent detection does not break existing keyword command handling
Type check passes
subtasks.output: detectIntent() classifies into tag_interest/help/greeting/question/unknown. eventRouter.handleUnregisteredUser() wired for users with no tags. Falls back gracefully on API errors.

subtasks.description: Add LLM-generated menus and dynamic help command
tasks: Add generateHelp() to llmService that produces context-aware help based on user tags and commands
Add generateWelcome() for unregistered user menu listing available programs
Update keywordHandler help command to delegate to llmService.generateHelp() when LLM enabled
Add setLlmService() to keywordHandler for dependency injection
Keep static fallback for when LLM is unavailable
verification: Help command returns LLM-generated text when LLM enabled
Help command returns static text when LLM disabled
Status command still admin-only (existing adminOnly gating unchanged)
Type check passes
subtasks.output: generateHelp() and generateWelcome() in llmService. keywordHandler delegates help to LLM with static fallback. Admin-only status command unchanged.

subtasks.description: Add generic greeting for unregistered users
tasks: Add handleUnregisteredUser() to eventRouter: detect intent → register or welcome
Wire LLM into both processEvent and routeEventSync no-route sections
Add generateUnregisteredResponse() for contextual replies to unregistered user questions
Wire llmService into eventRouter and keywordHandler in index.ts
Ensure tag keyword detection (SOMO etc.) still works for unregistered users
verification: Unregistered user with LLM enabled gets welcome with available programs
Unregistered user asking about a tag gets auto-registered via LLM intent
Unregistered user can still register via tag keyword (existing regex flow)
After registration, user gets tag-specific responses
All 22 test suites pass (2 known MongoDB failures)
docs/architecture/06-llm-conversational-system.md created
subtasks.output: Complete unregistered user flow: intent detection → auto-register or welcome. Wired in both sync and async event processing paths. 17 new LLM tests. Architecture doc created.

changelog: Added LLM-powered tag-based conversational system with intent detection, dynamic menus, and Grok model via OpenRouter

description: ERPNext sync adapter for shared operational schema
blocked_by: ERPNext instance at erp.dater.world (Phase E0 in docs/erpnext/ERP_SETUP_WA.md)

context.summary: ERPNext replaces planned Postgres tenancy as shared operational layer. wa.dt.wrld syncs users/campaigns/communications to ERPNext via REST API and receives real-time updates via webhooks. MongoDB remains the fast-path store; ERPNext is async write-through with periodic reconciliation.
Define ERPNext document interfaces and config. Spec has full field tables for Contact, Campaign, Communication DocTypes with custom fields.

tasks: Create src/types/erpnext.ts with ERPNextContact, ERPNextCampaign, ERPNextCommunication interfaces matching spec DocType field tables
Add ERPNextWebhookPayload type (event, doctype, name, data)
Add ERPNextSyncService interface as defined in spec
Add CommunicationLog type for batched message logging
Add erpnextConfig to shared/config.ts (ENABLE_ERPNEXT_SYNC, ERPNEXT_URL, API key/secret, webhook secret, sync intervals, company filter)
Add ERPNext lifecycle stage enum matching spec (Pending, Enrolled, Active, Completed, Alumni, Churned)
verification: npm run type-check passes
Types match spec/16 field tables exactly
Config defaults match spec (5min sync, 10s batch, disabled by default)
subtasks.output: ERPNext types in src/types/erpnext.ts, erpnextConfig in shared/config.ts

context.summary: Build HTTP client for Frappe REST API. Auth: token api_key:api_secret. CRUD at /api/resource/:doctype/:name. Filter syntax: [[field,op,value]].

tasks: Create src/services/erpnextClient.ts with typed HTTP methods for Frappe REST API
Implement createContact(), updateContact(), getContactByPhone() methods
Implement getActiveCampaigns() for bulk campaign loading with custom field selection
Implement createCommunication() for message logging
Add retry with exponential backoff for transient failures
Add request timeout (10s default) to prevent blocking
Log all ERPNext API calls at debug level, errors at error level
verification: npm run type-check passes
Unit tests with mocked fetch for each API method
Retry logic tested (3 attempts with backoff)
Auth header format correct: 'token key:secret'
subtasks.output: ERPNextClient class with typed CRUD methods, retry logic, and error handling

context.summary: Implement ERPNextSyncService interface. Write-through on user registration, batched communication logging, periodic cache refresh from ERPNext. Uses stateManager for MongoDB operations.

tasks: Create src/services/erpnextSync.ts implementing ERPNextSyncService interface
Implement initialize(): load all active Campaigns → tagConfigs, optionally Contacts → users
Implement syncUserRegistration(): write to MongoDB then queue ERPNext Contact create/update
Implement syncUserProgress() and syncLifecycleStage(): update ERPNext Contact custom fields
Implement logCommunication(): buffer messages, flush every ERPNEXT_BATCH_LOG_INTERVAL_MS
Implement refreshContactCache() and refreshCampaignCache(): periodic full refresh from ERPNext
Add graceful degradation: if ERPNext unreachable, log warning and continue with cached data
Add queue for failed syncs with retry backoff
verification: npm run type-check passes
Unit tests with mocked ERPNextClient for each sync method
Batching logic tested (accumulates then flushes)
Graceful degradation tested (ERPNext down → continues with cache)
Never blocks message routing (all ERPNext calls are fire-and-forget or background)
subtasks.output: ERPNextSyncService with async write-through, batched logging, and periodic refresh

context.summary: Express routes for ERPNext webhook events. Endpoints at /service/webhooks/erpnext/contact and /campaign. Validate X-Frappe-Webhook-Secret header. Delegate to sync service handlers.

tasks: Create src/routes/erpnextWebhooks.ts with Express router
Add POST /service/webhooks/erpnext/contact endpoint
Add POST /service/webhooks/erpnext/campaign endpoint
Add webhook secret validation middleware (X-Frappe-Webhook-Secret header)
Parse webhook payload (event, doctype, name, data) and delegate to sync service
Return 200 immediately, process async (webhook sender expects fast response)
Add request logging for webhook events
verification: npm run type-check passes
Unit tests with supertest for each webhook endpoint
Invalid secret returns 401
Valid webhook calls sync service handler methods
Returns 200 within 100ms (no blocking work in handler)
subtasks.output: Webhook receiver routes at /service/webhooks/erpnext/* with secret validation

subtasks.description: Wire sync into eventRouter and add integration tests

context.summary: Wire ERPNext sync into the main application. eventRouter fires sync events on user registration, tag assignment, message processing. index.ts initializes sync service on startup. Mount webhook routes.

tasks: Add setErpnextSync() to eventRouter for dependency injection
Fire syncUserRegistration() from eventRouter on new user registration (async, non-blocking)
Fire logCommunication() from eventRouter after message processing (async, batched)
Initialize ERPNextSyncService in index.ts when ENABLE_ERPNEXT_SYNC=true
Mount webhook routes in index.ts app setup
Start periodic cache refresh timer on initialization
Add integration tests: full flow from webhook → cache update → eventRouter reads updated data
Update docs/ROADMAP.md to mark ERPNext sync as in_progress
verification: npm run type-check passes
All existing tests still pass (sync disabled by default)
Integration test: mock ERPNext webhook → MongoDB cache updated
Integration test: user registration → sync queued to ERPNext
ENABLE_ERPNEXT_SYNC=false → no ERPNext code runs (zero overhead)
docs/ updated with ERPNext sync architecture notes
subtasks.output: ERPNext sync fully wired into event pipeline, feature-flagged, with integration tests

subtasks.description: One-time migration scripts for MongoDB → ERPNext seed

context.summary: Export existing MongoDB users and tagConfigs to ERPNext format for initial seed. Similar pattern to existing migrate-chatid-to-identifier.ts script.

tasks: Create scripts/export-users-for-erpnext.ts: read MongoDB users → output ERPNext Contact JSON
Create scripts/export-tagconfigs-for-erpnext.ts: read MongoDB tagConfigs → output ERPNext Campaign JSON
Map identifier/platform to Contact phone/custom_wa_platform fields
Map tag configs (targets, system prompt, welcome messages, regex) to Campaign custom fields
Add dry-run mode (output JSON without writing) and write mode (POST to ERPNext API)
Handle duplicates gracefully (skip if Contact already exists by phone)
verification: Export scripts produce valid ERPNext-compatible JSON
Dry-run outputs expected Contact/Campaign records for known test data
Write mode successfully creates records via ERPNext API (manual test with live instance)
subtasks.output: Migration scripts ready for one-time MongoDB → ERPNext seed

changelog: Added ERPNext sync adapter with async write-through, webhook receivers, and migration scripts
